response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins which are present in certain aquatic environments where peanuts might be introduced through human activity or contamination  
this reaction can lead to severe allergic responses such as anaphylaxis which can be life-threatening in any setting including underwater environments where medical assistance may not be readily available  

Sec. 2  
living underwater requires adaptation to a completely different ecosystem which includes exposure to various substances that may not be compatible with human physiology  
additionally the absence of medical facilities and the challenges of administering emergency treatment underwater further complicate the situation for individuals with severe allergies to peanuts
